description: Evals for 16 professional finance and compliance/legal skills

evals.prompt: Generate our income statement for Q4 2025
evals.expected_output: A formatted income statement covering revenue, COGS, gross profit, operating expenses, operating income, interest, taxes, and net income for Q4 2025 with margin percentages

assertions.text: Produces a structured income statement with revenue, COGS, gross profit, operating expenses, and net income line items
Includes margin percentages (gross margin, operating margin, net margin)
Uses use_skill with skill_name financial_statements_generation

evals.prompt: I need a full set of financial statements for the year ended December 2025 — income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow statement. Include common-size analysis and year-over-year comparisons against 2024.
evals.expected_output: All three core financial statements with common-size (vertical %) analysis and horizontal (YoY) comparisons showing dollar and percentage changes

assertions.text: Generates all three statements: income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow statement
Includes common-size analysis expressing line items as percentages of revenue (P&L) and total assets (BS)
Includes year-over-year dollar and percentage change comparisons
Uses use_skill with skill_name financial_statements_generation

evals.prompt: Prepare our balance sheet as of March 31 2026 with current vs non-current classification
evals.expected_output: A properly classified balance sheet separating current and non-current assets and liabilities, with shareholders equity section, verifying that total assets equal total liabilities plus equity

assertions.text: Separates assets and liabilities into current and non-current categories
Includes shareholders equity section with retained earnings
Uses use_skill with skill_name financial_statements_generation

evals.prompt: Analyze the revenue variance between our Q1 budget and actuals
evals.expected_output: A variance decomposition breaking the total revenue variance into price, volume, and mix components with narrative explanations for each driver

assertions.text: Decomposes total variance into price, volume, and mix components
Provides narrative explanations identifying root cause drivers
Uses use_skill with skill_name variance_analysis

evals.prompt: Our EMEA region missed its Q4 revenue target by $2M. Break down what happened — was it pricing, volume, product mix, or currency? We need a waterfall chart narrative and recommended corrective actions for the board.
evals.expected_output: Full variance decomposition with price, volume, mix, and FX components for EMEA, waterfall methodology, materiality assessment, root cause categorization, and corrective action recommendations

assertions.text: Includes FX variance analysis for the international region
Applies materiality thresholds and provides a waterfall analysis narrative
Recommends corrective actions for unfavorable structural variances
Uses use_skill with skill_name variance_analysis

evals.prompt: Compare our actual operating expenses against forecast for February and flag anything material
evals.expected_output: Expense variance analysis applying materiality thresholds, flagging items exceeding 10% or $50K, with root cause categories and manager explanations

assertions.text: Applies materiality thresholds to filter significant variances
Categorizes root causes (timing, volume, rate, one-time, structural)
Uses use_skill with skill_name variance_analysis

evals.prompt: Prepare the accrual journal entry for $15,000 in legal fees we received services for in February but won't be invoiced until March
evals.expected_output: A properly formatted accrual journal entry debiting Professional Fees Expense and crediting Accrued Liabilities for $15,000 with supporting documentation references

assertions.text: Follows double-entry accounting with debits equaling credits
Correctly identifies this as an accrual entry (expense recognized before cash payment)
Includes supporting documentation reference and description suitable for audit
Uses use_skill with skill_name journal_entry_preparation

evals.prompt: We collected $120,000 upfront for an annual SaaS subscription starting January 1. Prepare the initial collection entry and the monthly revenue recognition entry. Also prepare the depreciation entry for our new server equipment purchased for $60,000 with a 4-year straight-line useful life.
evals.expected_output: Three journal entries: (1) deferred revenue collection entry, (2) monthly revenue recognition entry for $10,000, and (3) monthly depreciation entry for $1,250, all with proper debit/credit format and references

assertions.text: Prepares a deferred revenue entry debiting Cash and crediting Deferred Revenue
Prepares monthly revenue recognition entry debiting Deferred Revenue and crediting Subscription Revenue
Calculates correct monthly depreciation amount using straight-line method
Includes approval workflow guidance based on entry amounts
Uses use_skill with skill_name journal_entry_preparation

evals.prompt: Help me plan our February month-end close process
evals.expected_output: A structured close calendar with pre-close, close period, and post-close tasks, owners, dependencies, and target completion days

assertions.text: Provides a day-by-day close calendar covering pre-close through post-close phases
Identifies task owners and dependencies between tasks
Uses use_skill with skill_name month_end_close_management

evals.prompt: Our month-end close is taking 15 business days. I need a plan to get it down to 7 days. Give me the full close checklist, identify critical path items, and suggest which tasks we can automate or parallelize.
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive close management plan with critical path analysis, pre/close/post checklists, KPIs, automation recommendations, and continuous improvement strategies targeting 7-day close

assertions.text: Identifies critical path items that gate downstream tasks (revenue cutoff, subledger closes, IC recon, tax provision, controller review)
Provides pre-close, close, and post-close checklists
Includes KPIs such as close duration target, error rate, and on-time task completion
Recommends automation and process improvements to shorten close duration
Uses use_skill with skill_name month_end_close_management

evals.prompt: Reconcile our main operating bank account for February
evals.expected_output: A bank reconciliation showing bank statement balance adjusted for deposits in transit and outstanding checks, GL balance adjusted for bank fees and interest, with both adjusted balances matching

assertions.text: Follows the standard bank reconciliation format adjusting both bank and book balances
Identifies reconciling items such as deposits in transit, outstanding checks, and bank fees
Uses use_skill with skill_name account_reconciliation

evals.prompt: We have a $45,000 discrepancy between our AP subledger and the GL control account, and several intercompany balances don't match between our US and UK entities. Walk me through the full reconciliation process, including aging analysis on the open items and escalation procedures.
evals.expected_output: Detailed reconciliation methodology for subledger-to-GL and intercompany reconciliation, including templates, aging buckets, auto-reconciliation rules, escalation thresholds, and three-way match procedures

assertions.text: Covers subledger-to-GL reconciliation process for accounts payable
Includes intercompany reconciliation with bilateral matching and tolerance levels
Applies aging analysis buckets (0-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days) with required actions
Specifies escalation thresholds based on dollar amounts and aging
Uses use_skill with skill_name account_reconciliation

evals.prompt: We need to test our revenue recognition controls for SOX compliance. What's the methodology?
evals.expected_output: SOX 404 testing methodology for revenue recognition controls including control identification, risk assessment, sample selection, testing procedures, and deficiency classification

assertions.text: Classifies controls as preventive vs detective and manual vs automated
Includes sample selection methodology based on population size and risk level
Uses use_skill with skill_name sox_testing_methodology

evals.prompt: Our external auditors found a potential control deficiency in our procurement three-way match process. Help me assess whether this is a deficiency, significant deficiency, or material weakness, and prepare the testing documentation to demonstrate remediation.
evals.expected_output: Deficiency classification analysis using SOX framework (deficiency vs significant deficiency vs material weakness), remediation testing plan with documentation standards, and sample selection methodology for re-testing

assertions.text: Applies the deficiency classification framework distinguishing deficiency, significant deficiency, and material weakness
Outlines testing procedures and documentation standards for demonstrating control remediation
Includes risk assessment tied to financial statement assertions
Uses use_skill with skill_name sox_testing_methodology

evals.prompt: Our external auditors are coming in two weeks. Help me prepare the PBC list and walkthrough documentation.
evals.expected_output: Audit preparation plan with PBC (Prepared by Client) list organized by audit area, walkthrough preparation templates, timeline, and coordination logistics

assertions.text: Provides a structured PBC list organized by audit area with document owners and due dates
Includes walkthrough preparation guidance for key processes
Uses use_skill with skill_name audit_support_framework

evals.prompt: Internal audit issued three findings from their Q4 review — two medium and one high severity. I need a remediation tracking framework, response templates for each finding, and a plan to prevent recurrence. Also help me prepare for the follow-up audit in 90 days.
evals.expected_output: Finding remediation framework with response templates, severity-based action plans, remediation tracking with milestones, root cause analysis methodology, and follow-up audit preparation checklist

assertions.text: Provides structured response templates for audit findings by severity level
Includes remediation tracking with milestones, owners, and target dates
Addresses root cause analysis and preventive controls to avoid recurrence
Outlines follow-up audit preparation steps and documentation requirements
Uses use_skill with skill_name audit_support_framework

evals.prompt: Review this vendor contract for our new cloud hosting provider
evals.expected_output: Systematic contract review covering key commercial terms, liability provisions, IP rights, termination clauses, and red flags with a risk assessment

assertions.text: Follows a structured review checklist covering commercial terms, liability, IP, and termination provisions
Identifies red flags and risk areas requiring negotiation
Uses use_skill with skill_name contract_review_framework

evals.prompt: We're about to sign a $2M enterprise software licensing agreement with a 3-year term. The vendor's paper has unlimited liability carve-outs, auto-renewal, and a broad IP assignment clause. Give me a full contract review with red flags, negotiation positions, and a recommended markup strategy. Turnaround needs to be within 48 hours.
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive contract review with red flag identification, negotiation playbook with fallback positions, markup recommendations, turnaround timeline adherence, and risk-prioritized action items

assertions.text: Identifies specific red flags including unlimited liability carve-outs, auto-renewal terms, and IP assignment concerns
Provides a negotiation playbook with recommended positions and fallback alternatives
Addresses turnaround standards and prioritizes review items by risk level
Uses use_skill with skill_name contract_review_framework

evals.prompt: We received an NDA from a potential partner. Can you triage it quickly?
evals.expected_output: NDA triage classification using GREEN/YELLOW/RED system comparing against standard template, with identified deviations and recommended actions

assertions.text: Classifies the NDA using the GREEN/YELLOW/RED triage system
Compares key terms against the company standard NDA template
Uses use_skill with skill_name nda_triage

evals.prompt: I have five NDAs that came in this week from different counterparties — two are on our paper, one is a mutual NDA on their paper, one has a non-standard jurisdiction clause, and one includes a non-solicitation rider. Triage all five and tell me which ones I can sign immediately, which need minor edits, and which require full legal review.
evals.expected_output: Batch NDA triage with individual GREEN/YELLOW/RED classifications, identification of deviations from standard terms (jurisdiction, non-solicitation), turnaround SLAs for each tier, and prioritized action queue

assertions.text: Assigns individual triage classifications (GREEN/YELLOW/RED) to each of the five NDAs
Identifies specific non-standard terms like jurisdiction clauses and non-solicitation riders as deviation triggers
Provides turnaround SLAs based on triage classification
Uses use_skill with skill_name nda_triage

evals.prompt: Assess the legal risk of expanding our operations into the EU market
evals.expected_output: Legal risk assessment using severity-likelihood matrix covering GDPR compliance, employment law, cross-border data transfer, IP protection, and regulatory obligations with mitigation strategies

assertions.text: Uses a severity-likelihood matrix to classify identified legal risks
Provides specific mitigation strategies for each identified risk
Uses use_skill with skill_name legal_risk_assessment

evals.prompt: We're considering acquiring a competitor that has two pending patent infringement lawsuits, a recent data breach, and operations in five countries with varying privacy regulations. Give me a comprehensive legal risk assessment with board reporting thresholds and a go/no-go recommendation framework.
evals.expected_output: Multi-dimensional legal risk assessment covering IP litigation exposure, data breach liability, multi-jurisdiction regulatory risk, severity-likelihood matrix, board reporting thresholds, and risk-adjusted deal recommendation

assertions.text: Assesses IP litigation risk from pending patent infringement lawsuits
Evaluates data breach liability and notification obligations
Maps multi-jurisdiction privacy regulation compliance requirements
Applies board reporting thresholds to determine escalation needs
Uses use_skill with skill_name legal_risk_assessment

evals.prompt: What are the legal risks of our new AI product that processes customer data?
evals.expected_output: Legal risk assessment covering AI-specific regulatory risks, data privacy (GDPR/CCPA), algorithmic bias liability, IP ownership of AI outputs, and consumer protection compliance

assertions.text: Identifies AI-specific legal risks including data privacy and algorithmic bias
Classifies risks by severity and likelihood with mitigation recommendations
Uses use_skill with skill_name legal_risk_assessment

evals.prompt: Run a compliance check before we launch our new mobile payment feature
evals.expected_output: Pre-launch compliance checklist covering regulatory requirements (PCI DSS, state money transmitter licenses), data privacy, consumer protection, and required approvals with go/no-go assessment

assertions.text: Provides a structured pre-launch compliance checklist organized by regulatory domain
Maps applicable regulations to specific business actions and features
Uses use_skill with skill_name compliance_check_framework

evals.prompt: We're rolling out a new employee monitoring software across all departments that tracks keystrokes and screen activity. Before deployment, I need a full compliance review covering employment law, privacy regulations in all our operating states, union considerations, and required employee notifications. Build me the approval workflow too.
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive compliance review covering employment law, state-by-state privacy variations, ECPA/CFAA considerations, union/collective bargaining obligations, employee notification requirements, and multi-level approval workflow

assertions.text: Maps applicable employment and privacy regulations across jurisdictions
Identifies employee notification and consent requirements
Provides a structured approval workflow with required sign-offs before deployment
Uses use_skill with skill_name compliance_check_framework

evals.prompt: Check the status of all our active vendor agreements
evals.expected_output: Vendor agreement inventory with key dates (expiration, renewal), compliance verification status, risk tiering, and flagged items requiring attention

assertions.text: Provides an inventory of vendor agreements with key dates and status
Applies risk tiering to categorize vendors by compliance exposure
Uses use_skill with skill_name vendor_agreement_check

evals.prompt: Three of our critical SaaS vendor contracts expire in the next 60 days. One vendor had a SOC 2 audit failure last quarter, another wants to increase pricing by 40%, and the third has been acquired by a competitor. Do a full vendor agreement health check, risk assessment, and give me a renewal/replacement recommendation for each.
evals.expected_output: Per-vendor assessment covering compliance verification (SOC 2 status), pricing analysis, change-of-control implications, risk tiering, and renewal vs replacement recommendations with transition planning

assertions.text: Assesses each vendor individually with compliance verification including SOC 2 audit status
Evaluates change-of-control and acquisition implications on contract terms
Provides renewal vs replacement recommendations with supporting rationale
Flags key dates and deadlines for action within the 60-day window
Uses use_skill with skill_name vendor_agreement_check

evals.prompt: Set up the e-signature routing for our new office lease agreement
evals.expected_output: E-signature preparation checklist with document verification, signing order sequence, signer identification, and routing configuration

assertions.text: Provides a pre-signature checklist verifying document completeness and authorization
Defines a signing order sequence with roles and routing logic
Uses use_skill with skill_name e_signature_routing

evals.prompt: We have a multi-party international agreement that needs signatures from our CEO in New York, our EU subsidiary director in Berlin, the counterparty's legal counsel in Tokyo, and a witness in London. Set up the e-signature routing with proper execution order, international compliance considerations (EU eIDAS, Japanese e-signature law), and fallback procedures if any signer is unavailable.
evals.expected_output: International e-signature routing plan with jurisdiction-specific compliance (eIDAS, Japanese Electronic Signatures Act), multi-party signing order, timezone-aware routing, authorized signatory verification, delegation/fallback procedures, and wet signature alternatives

assertions.text: Addresses international e-signature compliance requirements across jurisdictions (EU eIDAS, Japanese law)
Establishes a multi-party signing order with proper sequencing across four signers
Includes fallback procedures for unavailable signers and delegation authority
Uses use_skill with skill_name e_signature_routing

evals.prompt: Prepare a briefing for tomorrow's meeting with outside counsel about the pending employment dispute
evals.expected_output: Structured legal meeting briefing with agenda, background summary, risk assessment, recommended positions, key discussion points, and action item tracking template

assertions.text: Provides a structured agenda with time allocations and discussion topics
Includes risk assessment and recommended legal positions for the meeting
Uses use_skill with skill_name legal_meeting_briefing

evals.prompt: I have a board meeting Thursday where we need to discuss three legal matters: the ongoing patent litigation with Acme Corp (trial date set for June), the SEC inquiry into our Q3 disclosures, and the proposed acquisition of StartupCo which has regulatory approval pending in three jurisdictions. Prepare a comprehensive legal briefing for each item with risk ratings, recommended board positions, and decision points.
evals.expected_output: Multi-topic board-level legal briefing covering patent litigation status and trial preparation, SEC inquiry response strategy, and M&A regulatory approval status, each with risk ratings, recommended positions, decision points, and action tracking

assertions.text: Provides separate briefing sections for each of the three legal matters
Assigns risk ratings and recommended positions for board consideration
Identifies specific decision points and required board actions
Includes action item tracking with owners and deadlines
Uses use_skill with skill_name legal_meeting_briefing

evals.prompt: A customer is asking if they can use our API data in their marketing materials. How should I respond?
evals.expected_output: Templated legal inquiry response covering data usage rights under the API terms of service, licensing restrictions, required attributions, and escalation guidance if the request exceeds standard permissions

assertions.text: Provides a structured response addressing the specific legal inquiry about data usage rights
Includes escalation criteria if the inquiry requires attorney review
Uses use_skill with skill_name legal_inquiry_response

evals.prompt: We received four legal inquiries today: (1) an employee asking about their non-compete enforceability after resignation, (2) a vendor requesting indemnification scope clarification, (3) a customer claiming our product caused data loss and threatening litigation, and (4) a regulator requesting documentation on our data retention practices. Triage and draft responses for each with appropriate privilege markings and SLA tracking.
evals.expected_output: Four individually triaged legal inquiry responses with appropriate templates, privilege/confidentiality markings, SLA assignments based on urgency, escalation determinations, and tracking metadata

assertions.text: Triages each inquiry by urgency and complexity, with the litigation threat and regulator request receiving highest priority
Applies appropriate privilege markings (attorney-client privilege, work product) where applicable
Assigns SLAs based on inquiry type and urgency
Identifies which inquiries require escalation to senior counsel vs standard template responses
Uses use_skill with skill_name legal_inquiry_response

evals.prompt: Generate a legal briefing on the new state privacy laws taking effect this quarter
evals.expected_output: Contextual legal briefing covering new state privacy legislation, key requirements, compliance gaps, impact assessment, and recommended action items with deadlines

assertions.text: Provides a structured legal briefing on the specified regulatory topic
Identifies compliance gaps and recommends specific action items
Uses use_skill with skill_name legal_briefing_generation

evals.prompt: I need three legal briefings: (1) a daily legal update summarizing overnight regulatory developments and case law changes, (2) a deep-dive research briefing on the implications of the EU AI Act for our product roadmap, and (3) an incident response briefing for the data breach we discovered yesterday affecting 50,000 customer records. Each briefing should be formatted appropriately for its audience and urgency level.
evals.expected_output: Three distinct legal briefings in appropriate formats: daily update digest with overnight developments, deep-dive research memo on EU AI Act product implications, and urgent incident response briefing with notification timelines, regulatory obligations, and immediate action steps

assertions.text: Generates three distinct briefing types (daily update, topic research, incident response) with format appropriate to each
The incident response briefing includes notification timelines, regulatory obligations, and immediate action steps appropriate to a data breach affecting 50,000 records
The EU AI Act research briefing covers product roadmap implications with specific compliance requirements
Each briefing is tailored to its audience and urgency level
Uses use_skill with skill_name legal_briefing_generation
